name: Vulhub-nginx-insecure-configuration
isolation_note: Vulhub nginx/insecure-configuration (off-by-slash alias misconfig on :8081 -> mapped to host 8097), bound to 127.0.0.1 only. Owned, disposable local lab. Ports 8096/8098 are the redirect/CSP misconfigs (not file-read).
rationale: nginx off-by-slash alias traversal (`location /files { alias /home/; }` -> /files../ == /home/.. == /). Real misconfig fingerprint for the information-leak / directory-traversal family (① + ④), which had NO Vulhub representative before. Process = enumerate (autoindex /files/) -> discover the off-by-slash -> read /etc/passwd. nginx has NO command execution, so this is a FILE-READ-MILESTONE box (like php-inclusion / rails): the full cmd-exec+file goal is unreachable by design; it reaches the read_files milestone only. Payload to be empirically confirmed.
